data.title: Taylor Morrison Home Corporation Non-GAAP EPS of $2.37 beats by $0.33, revenue of $2.12B beats by $150M
data.description:
• Taylor Morrison Home Corporation press release (NYSE:TMHC): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $2.37 beats by $0.33.
  • Revenue of $2.12B (+26.2% Y/Y) beats by $150M.
  • Net sales orders increased 9% to 2,830
  • Home closings revenue of $2.0 billion, driven by 3,394 closings at an average price of $598,000
  • 83,579 homebuilding lots owned and controlled, of which a record 58% was controlled off balance sheet

  • Business Outlook

    Fourth Quarter 2024

    • Home closings are expected to be approximately 3,400
    • Average closing price is expected to be approximately $610,000
    • Home closings gross margin is expected to be around 24.5%
    • Ending active community count is expected to be between 330 to 340
    • Effective tax rate is expected to be approximately 25%
    • Diluted share count is expected to be approximately 106 million

  • Full Year 2024

    • Home closings are now expected to be approximately 12,725
    • Average closing price is now expected to be approximately $600,000
    • Home closings gross margin is now expected to be approximately 24.3%
    • Ending active community count is expected to be between 330 to 340
    • SG&A as a percentage of home closings revenue is expected to be in the high-9% range
    • Effective tax rate is now expected to be between 24.5% to 25.0%
    • Diluted share count is expected to be approximately 107 million
    • Land and development spend is now expected to be around $2.5 billion
    • Share repurchases are expected to total approximately $300 million

data.title: Tutor Perini subsidiary bags $1.1B connection tunnel project
data.description:
• Tutor Perini (NYSE:TPC) said that its subsidiary, Frontier-Kemper Constructors has been awarded a contract valued at ~$1.1 billion by the New York City Department of Environmental Protection for the Kensico-Eastview Connection Tunnel Project.
  • Work is expected to begin in the coming months with substantial completion expected in 2030.
  • The contract value was added to the company’s backlog in Q3 2024.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Thermo Fisher Scientific tops Q3 profit estimates, forecast misses
MakeMyTrip Non-GAAP EPS of $0.36, revenue of $168.7M
data.description:
• MakeMyTrip press release (NASDAQ:MMYT): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.36.
  • Revenue of $168.7M (-20.0% Y/Y).
  • Gross Bookings increased by 24.3% YoY in 2Q25 to $2,257.2 million.
    •
    Adjusted Margin – Air Ticketing increased by 21.1% YoY in 2Q25 to $96.0 million.
    •
    Adjusted Margin – Hotels and Packages increased by 21.4% YoY in 2Q25 to $90.7 million.
    •
    Adjusted Margin – Bus Ticketing increased by 25.6% YoY in 2Q25 to $27.1 million.
    •
    Adjusted Margin – Others increased by 51.0% YoY in 2Q25 to $16.4 million.
    •
    Adjusted Operating Profit improved to $37.5 million in 2Q25 versus $28.2 million in 2Q24, reflecting an improvement of $9.3 million YoY.
    •
    Adjusted Net Profit improved to $40.7 million in 2Q25 versus $27.8 million in 2Q24, reflecting an improvement of $12.9 million YoY.

data.title: Stock index futures slip, yields tick higher as investors await Tesla earnings
data.description:
Stock index futures slipped on Wednesday, a day after the S&P 500 index posted back-to-back negative sessions for the first time since early September. Investors will now turn their attention to Tesla (TSLA) earnings.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) -0.2%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) -0.2% and Dow futures (INDU) -0.4%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 1 basis point to 4.23%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 2 basis points to 4.06%.

Wall Street ended in a mixed fashion on Tuesday, with the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite finishing higher.

"The markets are being weighed down by the recent increase in bond rates and declining hope on the direction of the interest rate," Patrick Munnelly, from Tickmill Group, said.

"With less than two weeks to go before the Nov. 5 election, investors are bracing for volatility in the markets," Munnelly added.

Investors will also keep their eyes on Tesla's (TSLA) Q3 earnings report on Wednesday, which will land after the closing bell.

On the economic front, the September existing home sales report will land during market hours, and is expected to come in at 3.88M.

"We think existing home sales were little changed last month, although a small decline is more likely than an increase," Pantheon Macroeconomics said.

The Fed's Beige Book will be released later in the day.

data.title: Eli Lilly’s Alzheimer’s drug donanemab rejected for NHS use
data.description:
A U.K. watchdog has declined to endorse Eli Lilly’s (NYSE:LLY) Alzheimer’s drug donanemab for use in the country's national health service (NHS).

The National Institute for Health and Care Excellence (NICE), which sets drug policy for the NHS, issued a draft guidance on Wednesday, noting that the use of donanemab “does not currently demonstrate value for the NHS and cannot be considered good value for the taxpayer.”

“The cost-effectiveness estimate for donanemab is 5 to 6 times above what NICE normally considers an acceptable use of NHS resources,” NICE said. It is estimated that around 70,000 adults in England would have been eligible for treatment with donanemab.

Donanemab, an injection for intravenous infusion every four weeks, in indicated to treat mild cognitive impairment and mild dementia due to Alzheimer's disease in eligible adults.

The rebuke came shortly after the U.K. drug regulator, the Medicines and Healthcare Products Regulatory Agency (MHRA), approved the drug, making Great Britain the third major market where donanemab has received approval. The therapy was first approved in the U.S. in July 2024 and then approved in Japan in September 2024.

In the U.S., donanemab treatment costs ~$78,000 per year when necessary scans and monitoring are factored in.

This marks the second such rejection in the U.K. for an Alzheimer’s therapy, after Biogen (BIIB) and Eisai's (OTCPK:ESAIY) Alzheimer's drug Leqembi met a similar fate earlier this year. In August, NICE issued a draft recommendation that the NHS not cover lecanemab because its benefits were too small to justify the cost.

data.title: Constellium GAAP EPS of €0.02, revenue of €1.6B
data.description:
• Constellium press release (NYSE:CSTM): Q3 GAAP EPS of €0.02.
  • Revenue of €1.6B (-5% Y/Y)
  • Shipments of 352 thousand metric tons, down 5% compared to Q3 2023.
  • Adjusted EBITDA of €110 million
    • Includes negative €17 million impact at Valais as a result of the flood
    • Includes negative non-cash metal price lag impact of €3 million

data.title: Travel+Leisure Non-GAAP EPS of $1.57 beats by $0.08, revenue of $993M misses by $17M
data.description:
• Travel+Leisure press release (NYSE:TNL): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.57 beats by $0.08.
  • Revenue of $993M (+0.7% Y/Y) misses by $17M.
  • Volume per guest (VPG) above $3,000 on a 4 percent increase in tours year-over-year, with new owner tours up 9 percent

  • The Company is providing its fourth quarter 2024 guidance:

    • Adjusted EBITDA of $240 million to $260 million
    • Gross VOI sales of $550 million to $600 million
    • VPG of $2,900 to $3,000
    • Travel and Membership Adjusted EBITDA of $45 million to $50 million

  • The Company is updating its previous guidance for the 2024 full year:

    • Adjusted EBITDA of $915 million to $935 million (no change from prior outlook)
    • Gross VOI sales of $2.25 billion to $2.30 billion (vs. prior outlook of $2.25 billion to $2.35 billion)
    • VPG of $3,000 to $3,025 (vs. prior outlook of $2,950 to $3,050)
